scenario_brief: You are a cyber security student tasked with recovering the Professor's backup of the CyBOK LaTeX source files for the CyBOK 1.1 release. According to legend, the HDD is stored in a safe in the Professor's office. Follow the clues scattered around the department to find the safe code. Time to put your physical security skills to the test! Good luck, and try not to get caught... or expelled.
endGoal: Recover the CyBOK LaTeX source HDD

objects.name: Reception Phone
objects.voice: Welcome to the Computer Science Department! The CyBOK backup is in the Professor's safe. The door through to the offices is also locked, so I guess it's safe for now.
objects.observations: The reception phone plays back a voicemail message

objects.name: Lockpicking Hint Notice
objects.text:
DEPARTMENT NOTICE:

Many doors use pin tumbler locks.

Tip: Apply tension to the lock while manipulating the pins with a pick.

Do NOT attempt this in front of colleagues.
objects.observations: A cautionary notice about lockpicking techniques

objects.name: Reception Computer
objects.observations: The receptionist's computer with a visible sticky note

contents.name: Building Directory
contents.text: Reminder: Leaving passwords on sticky notes is a bad security practice. Always use a password manager!

contents.name: Crypto Analysis Station
contents.observations: A powerful workstation for cryptographic analysis

objects.name: Overflowing Trash Bin
objects.observations: A messy trash bin overflowing with papers and debris

contents.name: Crumpled Meeting Notes
contents.text:
OVERHEARD CONVERSATION:

Admin: 'The Professor keeps losing his keys!'
IT Director: 'Why not put a key backup somewhere secure?'
Admin: 'I threw the office key in this bin. He always finds it eventually...'

(Obviously this was a bad plan)

contents.name: Admin Office Key
contents.observations: A dusty key marked 'ADMIN' - retrieved from garbage. Gross.

objects.name: Old Messenger Bag
objects.observations: A worn messenger bag sitting in the corner, partially open

contents.name: Safe Code Hint - CyBOK Reference
contents.text:
SAFE CODE CLUE #1:

CyBOK 1.1 defines several key domains:

🔐 Security Fundamentals
🔐 Software Security
🔐 System Architecture
🔐 Cryptography
🔐 Systems Security
🔐 Network Security
🔐 Cyber-Physical Systems
🔐 Cloud Security
🔐 Distributed Systems
🔐 Security Operations
🔐 Organizational & Human Aspects

Count them all...
contents.observations: A reference card with CyBOK domains listed

contents.name: Post-It: Professor's Password Hint
contents.text:
PASSWORD HINT:

The Professor's laptop password is:
'cybok' + (current year)

Example password: cybok2024
Base64 encoded: Y3lib2syMDI0

(He uses this same password everywhere. Very secure.)
contents.observations: A helpful post-it note about password patterns

objects.name: Admin Computer
objects.observations: The admin office computer with a visible sticky note password

contents.name: Safe Configuration Log
contents.text:
SAFE ACCESS LOG:

- Safe Owner: Professor
- Safe Location: Professor's Office, North wall
- Lock Type: Digital PIN
- Code: Based on educational reference
- Code Hint: 'Think about how many major topics I teach'

The code is mathematical - count something important to CyBOK.

objects.name: Professor's Laptop
objects.observations: The Professor's laptop with a Base64-encoded password on a sticky note. This is a cryptography challenge!

contents.name: Safe Code Hint - DECODED
contents.text:
SAFE CODE DECODED:

I've written the safe code down after forgetting it twice.

The code represents: How many Knowledge Areas does CyBOK 1.1 have?

Quick count from my syllabus:
- Foundational Concepts
- Software Development
- System Design
- Cryptography
- Systems Security
- Network Security
- Cyber-Physical
- Cloud Security
- Distributed Systems
- Security Operations
- Human & Organizational

That's... 11 areas!

Safe Code: 11

objects.name: CyBOK 1.1 Syllabus
objects.text:
CYBOK 1.1 COURSE SYLLABUS

Module Schedule:

1️⃣ Security and Privacy Risks
2️⃣ Secure Software Development
3️⃣ System Architecture
4️⃣ Cryptography Fundamentals
5️⃣ Systems Security
6️⃣ Network Security
7️⃣ Cyber-Physical Systems
8️⃣ Cloud Security
9️⃣ Distributed Systems Security
🔟 Security Operations
1️⃣1️⃣ Human, Organisational & Regulatory

Total Coverage: 11 Major Knowledge Areas
objects.observations: The Professor's CyBOK course syllabus with all 11 knowledge areas clearly listed

objects.name: Wall Safe
objects.observations: A secure wall safe with a digital PIN lock

contents.name: CyBOK LaTeX Source HDD
contents.text:
🎉 SUCCESS! 🎉

You have successfully recovered the CyBOK 1.1 LaTeX source files!

Contained on this HDD:
✅ 11 Knowledge Area modules
✅ 350+ pages of security goodness
✅ Comprehensive CyBOK framework documentation
✅ 47 diagrams (the Professor spent way too long on these)
✅ One very grumpy Professor's entire weekend

🚩 flag{cybok_heist_success}

Mission accomplished! Now get out before anyone notices!
contents.observations: The legendary CyBOK LaTeX source HDD - your ticket to freedom!

objects.name: Professor's Desk Calendar
objects.text:
PROFESSOR'S CALENDAR:

Monday: CyBOK lecture prep
Tuesday: More CyBOK prep
Wednesday: Even more CyBOK prep
Thursday: Did I mention CyBOK?
Friday: Coffee and crying about LaTeX formatting

P.S. - Finally got all 11 knowledge areas in the curriculum!
objects.observations: A desk calendar showing the Professor's obsession with CyBOK
